input.question: What is the refund policy for enterprise customers?

chunks.text: Enterprise customers are eligible for a full refund within 30 days of initial purchase. After 30 days, refunds are prorated based on the remaining contract period. To initiate a refund, contact billing@movate.io with your account ID and the reason for cancellation.

Annual contract customers who cancel mid-term receive a prorated credit applied to their next invoice rather than a cash refund. Refund exceptions may be granted at the discretion of the Finance team for documented service outages exceeding SLA thresholds.

input.question: How does the platform handle SOC 2 compliance and data encryption?

chunks.text: Movate maintains SOC 2 Type II certification, audited annually by an independent third party. All data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 and in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher. Encryption keys are managed via AWS KMS with automatic rotation every 365 days.

Data residency options are available for Enterprise tier customers in the EU, US, and APAC regions. Customer data is never used for model training or shared with third parties without explicit written consent.
Penetration testing is conducted annually by an approved third-party vendor. Results and remediation timelines are available to customers under NDA upon request.

input.question: What are the pricing tiers and what features does each include?

chunks.text: Starter tier: up to 5 users, 10k API calls/month, community support, 99.5% uptime SLA. $49/month billed annually.
Professional tier: up to 25 users, 100k API calls/month, email + chat support (business hours), 99.9% uptime SLA, SSO via SAML/OIDC, audit logs. $299/month billed annually.

Enterprise tier: unlimited users, custom API call limits, 24/7 dedicated support, 99.99% uptime SLA, custom domain, IP allowlisting, data residency, and MSA/SLA customization. Pricing negotiated annually.

input.question: Can I export my data for a GDPR data access request?

chunks.text: Customers may submit data subject access requests (DSARs) on behalf of their end users via the Subject Data Export API endpoint. The response is a signed S3 URL to a ZIP archive containing all records, audit logs, and uploaded files referencing the subject. Delivery is within 24 hours of request.

For right-to-erasure (GDPR Article 17) requests, use the /api/v1/subjects/erase endpoint. This is irreversible and will purge the subject's records from all backups after the 30-day retention window expires.

input.question: What happens when I exceed my monthly API call limit?

chunks.text: When an account exceeds its monthly API call limit, additional calls return HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header. Overage charges apply at $0.001 per excess call for Professional tier. Enterprise customers can negotiate hard caps (no overage) or a higher included limit in their MSA.
